description:
All per-tool CLI launch arguments are hardcoded in `build_cli_launch_contract()` in runtime_contract.rs (lines 130-255). Tool-specific flag injection (--read-only, --response-schema) is hardcoded in phase_executor.rs via `if cli_name == "oai-runner"` checks.

Adding a new tool or changing flags requires editing multiple Rust files. Solution: Move tool definitions (executable name, base args, supports_write, supports_vision, context_window, response_schema_flag, read_only_flag) into the agent-runtime config. The code reads tool definitions from config and builds launch contracts generically.
title: Make CLI launch args and tool capabilities config-driven instead of hardcoded match arms
